programme.title: Emergency
programme.desc: Emergency returns as a tense four-part serial across the week. As a pedestrian is crushed by a bus and a holiday paraglide goes wrong, can the frontline trauma units save lives?

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver shows us how to make a super-fast, flavoursome smoky noodle soup, the cleverest home-made bread and a chicken and mushroom pie with a time-saving tip for perfect pastry.

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Susie and Bill start converting a small Devon barn and try to make the best use of space, while Dannie and Grace prepare to welcome Barry the boar.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Joanne hosts a singles night at the Best of British pub near Orlando. Andreas wants to expand his bootcamp brand. And Holly ventures into historical costumes to grow her business.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, the Garners help Bob and Michelle find their perfect retirement property. In Murcia, the Browns are on the lookout for a coastal gem for Michelle and Steve.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sandra and Desmond are looking for their dream holiday home near Mar Menor's stunning beaches on Spain's Costa Calida. Can Danni Menzies find the perfect property for their £150,000 budget?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mark has a lifelong love of Italy, and having recently retired, he's set his sights on a bolthole in Puglia. Can Laura Hamilton find him a perfect place for his £80k?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe's helping Claire from Bedfordshire find a holiday home in the heart of Mallorca's capital, Palma. Claire has a budget of £190,000 and Craig has five great properties to show her.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Alison and Zion race to finish a wedding garden makeover. Tim and Krys get stuck with eight tonnes of quick drying concrete. Tim and Sasha upgrade their chicken coop.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Christine and Howard have decided to fulfil their dream of buying a place in the historic town of Mojácar in Spain's Almería region. Can Lee Juggurnauth find the perfect place for £50k?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The week's new competition is in Surrey, where the first to host is 38-year-old chauffeur James, who is eager to bring a taste of the tropics to Surrey with his Caribbean inspired menu.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second host in Surrey is nail technician Myle, who hopes her traditional Vietnamese food will help bag her the prize. Extra pressure is applied when Myle's mother shows up in the kitchen.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third to host in Surrey is 35-year-old hairdresser Ben, who's hoping to wow his guests by putting on a Mexican fiesta. Find out whether a night of mariachis and pinatas will win his guests over.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is day five of the competition in Surrey, and uber-competitive businessman John is banking on his 100 home cooked menu to bag him the prize.

programme.title: World's Biggest Curry Restaurant
programme.desc: What does it take to run the largest Pakistani Indian restaurant on earth? We go behind the scenes of the Royal Nawaab in Greater Manchester to see how they deliver perfection at scale.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: A family of three dogs are split up to increase their chances of finding a home. Darcy the cockapoo calls out for a hero and may just find one in 11-year-old Luke.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The fresh batch of teams face the now-iconic secret challenge and this time, it's the classic Paris Brest, with a twist. For their next challenge, the teams create childhood-toy-inspired showpieces concealing crumble and custard desserts.

programme.title: Marilyn and the Mob
programme.desc: Marilyn Monroe was the brightest star of fifties Hollywood. Beneath the glitz was an industry shaped by the mob. Did Marilyn pay dearly in a brutal game of power and betrayal?

programme.title: Marilyn and the Mob
programme.desc: Marilyn Monroe travels to Nevada with Frank Sinatra, placing her amid mobsters and dangerous rumours. Days later, she's found dead. Was her death just an accident?

programme.title: She Said
programme.desc: The New York Times journalists Megan Twohey and Jodi Kantor publish a report that exposes decades of sexual abuse allegations against powerful Hollywood producer Harvey Weinstein.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Will there be chemistry between two self-confessed nerds when engineer Ellie meets nuclear project manager Callum? Author Beckie meets poet Paul. And disco DJ John's love life's been stuck on pause...

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Derbyshire's finest face a series of issues with motorcyclists - with one evading them for over an hour in a high-speed chase.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jacko is hot on the heels of a speeding Mazda, while sparks also fly as the interceptors raid a cannabis farm. Spike and Richie pursue a suspected drug dealer, and canine cop Shadow sniffs out stolen goods.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Liam and police dog Titan are tracking suspects in a full-scale manhunt. Paul takes two drug dealers by surprise, and a man needs to be rescued from the River Wear. In Darlington, Damo finds a man unconscious after a punch-up.

programme.title: The Big Trees
programme.desc: Jim Fallon, an unscrupulous lumber baron goes to northern California to part the Quaker settlers from their giant sequoias. However, the homesteaders are having none of it, and Fallon ends up falling for one of the Friends.

programme.title: Entertainment News On 5
programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: The Proud Rebel
programme.desc: At the end of the Civil War, a Southerner travels north, determined to find a cure for his mute son.

programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: The Proud Rebel
programme.desc: At the end of the Civil War, a Southerner travels north, determined to find a cure for his mute son.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: When a loaded HGV suffers a puncture on a busy motorway, Crouch recovery op Jim Howard and heavy vehicle mechanic Jack Watson are called to the scene. What begins as a simple job, soon turns complicated.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The road crime team tail a wanted driver in their unmarked cars. They plan to surround the target.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A stick of dynamite is brought into Carlisle's main police station, a gun is spotted in a city-centre bar and Duncan receives a shock when a cyclist who has been crushed and trapped under a car turns out to be someone he knows.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Cumbria's elite interceptor teams are called out to deal with a suspect gunman, a pedestrian under a car and to destroy a cannabis farm, and a Reliant Robin is bid a fiery farewell.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Cases include a driver without a licence whose car reeks of cannabis, a man who can't spell the name he gives to officers, and boy racers causing chaos in a car park.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Profiling the work of high-speed police interception units around the country with some of the most dangerous pursuits and shocking footage ever captured. Exploring what it takes to bring order to chaos.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: A police helicopter is sent to the scene after a car stolen in Gloucester is crashed by a pair of fleeing youths. Meanwhile, a night worker in a petrol station is attacked by a customer.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: The agents uncover more information in the government's attempt to hide the truth about extraterrestrial life when Mulder discovers a video of an alleged alien autopsy.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: The most heavily-guarded prison in Croatia, where 500 criminals serve their sentences here, experience the struggle for survival every day begins anew.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Veteran road train trucker Mark Pett must haul 78 tonnes of cement and steel through the Northern Territory's wet season.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A 38-tonne mail lorry crashes on a London street; a bashed-up Audi is wedged in a ditch on a busy country road; a former US military vehicle is turned into a recovery machine.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Super-sized transport jobs require even bigger trucks to perform them. Lifting the lid on the heavy recovery business, featuring industrial strength trucks capable of towing and lifting the biggest and heaviest machines on the nation's roads.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: The Beast from the East descends, covering parts of the UK in a blanket of snow and ice and bringing some of the country's busiest roads to a standstill. Phones are ringing off the hook at as scores of drivers are left stranded on Britain's roads.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jacko and the firearms unit flush out a suspect after finding a homemade gun, while a 999 call leads to a drugs bust for Liam, and dog handler Justin sniffs out the truth when a mum and son tell some porkies over who a crashed a car.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jacko and the Firearms team are called to house where a suspected shotgun has been seen, after a short stand-off, the situation soon descends into something more sinister when the team discover what could be a home-made bomb.

programme.title: The First of the Few
programme.desc: Absorbing film biography of R J Mitchell, the designer of the Spitfire, the aircraft that enabled the Royal Air Force to defeat the might of Germany's Luftwaffe during the Battle of Britain.

programme.title: The First of the Few

programme.title: Raid on Rommel
programme.desc: A British Army intelligence officer, posing as a commando chief, leads a group of soldiers on an intentionally unsuccessful raid behind enemy lines. Once imprisoned in a POW camp, he turns his attention to escape and the aim of his assignment - to carry out a surprise attack on General Rommel's strategically placed artillery emplacements.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A digger has fallen off the back of a lorry and rolled over, blocking one side of a busy A road. Richard Crouch and recovery op Lee Brown have a hay bale of a time trying to get a valuable straw load on a broken down trailer back safely to its owner.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Derbyshire's finest face a series of issues with motorcyclists - with one evading them for over an hour in a high-speed chase.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A serial offender gives officers the runaround, a high-speed pursuit comes to a painful finish, the occupants of a 4x4 need rescuing after overturning on treacherous ice, and the interceptors are called on a mucky mercy mission after two of their own get caught in a sticky situation.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Angus and Sean have to smash down a door in order to save a man's life, veteran interceptor Gordon picks up a gruesome twosome on the M6, and a fleeing Land Rover rams two cop cars in a bid to get away.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: On the nightshift in Newcastle, PCs Jan Simlesa and Jamie Leslie spot a driver with his foot down in a top of the range BMW. Meanwhile, a force-wide hunt for two stolen cars leads Jan, Marc and Jamie to the seaside town of Whitley Bay.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Twenty-two year-old Steph Custance tries to complete the first ice roads mission of her career, but a crossing that's just opened pushes her to the edge.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Scully and Mulder's situation becomes increasingly dangerous when a mysterious government stranger enters the picture.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: Taichung Men's Prison is the biggest in Taiwan, housing some of the most dangerous criminals and operating a system of progressive correctional treatment.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Veteran trucker Yogi goes on a mercy mission for the drought ravaged farmers of New South Wales. He is leading a hay convoy.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: An 18-wheel trailer catches fire and closes 3 lanes just before rush hour; an 18-tonne lorry loses traction and smashes into a tree; a delivery truck gets stuck in the mud.

programme.desc: Nigel's open-top bus tour hits a snag, Ant preps for a trip home, and Aruna's Indian cookery classes and Bollywood night test her friends' (and clients') taste for spice and fun!

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Shane launches a bingo night at his Torremolinos bar. Kirstie road-trips with Minnie Tickle to see her villa, and Bambi & Johnny dust off their dancing shoes for a fundraiser.

programme.title: Fare Dodgers: At War With The Law
programme.desc: In Ilford undercover Investigators try to catch a suspected fare dodger they believe has racked up over £3000 in unpaid fares on nearly 500 journeys, misusing contactless.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Following the work of the North Yorkshire Police on the frontline of Britain's roads, where the fight against crime is fast and furious.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: On the lookout for a high-value car that's been taken from a dealership during a test drive. A builder's van is taken from a house driveway while its owner pops briefly inside to collect his tools and equipment.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The traffic cops are alerted when a car is spotted by police cameras being driven with no insurance. PC Andy Swift stops a car after suspecting it's not insured. The driver gets straight out and is very aggressive.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Tim is almost mown down by a desperate driver, Adam's patience is tested to the limit by a drunken road-hog, and Ange gets spooked while on the lookout for a prowler, and a group of youths gets one over on the interceptors.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A gang of lorry thieves trigger a major manhunt, a young man is busted for heroin possession, a fraudster is taken into custody and a helicopter pursuit ends in an almighty accident.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A petrol thief runs out of gas, the helicopter follows a car thief to the fair and a drunk driver does not have to travel far to be patched up when he crashes into a surgery.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: A jewellery claim raises suspicion when a woman provides too much paperwork, while CCTV catches a conman deliberately crashing his car to inflate a minor rear-end shunt. (S2 Ep18)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Remi accompanies Sonny back to the Surf Club from his physio session.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Over coffee, Remi apologises to Eden - she was right that he does tend to self-sabotage.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Dr Razak's first patient of the day is Muriel, an elderly woman who recently suffered a mini-stroke. She is reluctant to take her blood pressure medication because she has an aversion to drugs.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: New to the department, Doctor Martha Ellis-Parkinson is thrown in at the deep end, as paramedics bring in a 78-year-old former miner who is suffering with chest pain.

programme.title: Angels & Demons
programme.desc: Having ruffled the feathers of the Catholic Church with his controversial investigations into Christian history, Harvard symbologist Robert Langdon is surprised to be approached by the Pope's emissaries for help. Following the Pope's recent death, the four candidates most likely to take the pontiff's place have been kidnapped by a mysterious organisation known as the Illuminati.

programme.desc: Relaxing images and gentle, classical music.

programme.title: Mini Matinee
programme.desc: The loveable animal friends visit the Wild West. Pioneers park their wagon in a canyon and meet Native Americans. Everyone is having fun as they are toasting marshmallows around the bonfire.

programme.title: Songs & Rhymes
programme.desc: When we meet somebody everywhere we go, we say hello. Ciao, Konnichi wa, Marhaba - Hello has different names - in Italian, Japanese or Arabic. Let's greet our friends from all around the world with a nice hello in their language.

programme.title: Walter & Dude
programme.desc: Walter and Dude are looking for an item that protects eyes from the sun and that you look through. Is it a parasol or a magnifying glass? Join them as they discover sunglasses!

programme.title: Happy Birthday to You

programme.title: Bath Tubbies
programme.desc: Ellie, Frogy and Ducky splash in the water and have adventures while playing in the bath. They float around, play with funny bubbles and invent new games with soap and shampoo bottles.

programme.title: Draco
programme.desc: Join Draco as he learns about opposites by comparing sizes of chairs. One is too short, the other is too tall, but at the end, he receives a suitable chair that is just the right size so that he can reach the table and enjoy his tasty cake!

programme.title: The World Is A Wonder
programme.desc: Gaia the fairy takes toddlers to discover the magical changes in nature such as the life cycle of animals, the seasons of the year and how caterpillars turn into butterflies.

programme.title: Zeina's Detective Agency
programme.desc: A pan arrives at Zeina's doorstep, forgetting what it is used for. Using her detective skill and her best friend Roy, Zeina discovers that a pan is used for playing tennis... is it?

programme.title: Welcome to the Island
programme.desc: It's an exciting day on the island when a turtle and her numerous kids arrive on the island. In all the commotion, mama turtle finds herself flat on her back! Come see how she gets herself out of this predicament.

programme.title: Teeny and Tiny's Classroom
programme.desc: Teeny and Tiny are two happy and mischievous friends that share with us their riddles and games. As we follow the antics of the tiny friends, we discover shapes, colours and quantities.

programme.title: Mike's Bike
programme.desc: Tommy is having a hard time keeping his balance while riding a bike. After trying some of Mike's ideas, he manages to ride with training wheels.

programme.desc: Documenting the place of village halls at the heart of communities across Scotland throughout the 20th century. programme.title: Murder on the Victorian Railway
programme.desc: London 1864. On a Hackney bound train, a guard discovers blood in a first class carriage - and a body on the railway embankment. For the first time, a murder has taken place on Britain's railways. Over a hundred years later, this documentary uses the first hand testimony of the people involved at the time to explore this unique event and how it provoked a huge public outcry and debate.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie's juggling college and family life, learning about teenage mental health, online pressures and autism - and how care reaches far beyond the classroom.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Coles joins Robson to explore the Northumberland coastline. They take a Canadian canoe trip along the river Coquet, visit the hidden gem of Walkworth's secret hermitage, and make the crossing to Lindisfarne for a photography class.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In Somerset, Mickey from the busy seaside town of Hove is ready to fulfil a long-held dream of escaping to the countryside. She wants a property with lots of land for her big, beloved rescue dog, and Jules Hudson is there to answer her prayers with a line-up of standout properties that fit the bill. Jules also tries his hand at traditional screen printing when he visits a small studio specialising in designs inspired by nature.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and Flo head to Shrewsbury to help James and his sister Maria transform his plain, uninspired garden into a Doctor Who-inspired sci-fi dream.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team - a family of four, Anna, Gemma, Zoe, and Andreas- take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category.

programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.desc: Amanda finds out that a big house on her street is owned by an old lady who is planning to move out. So, she inserts herself into her life, hoping to get first refusal on the sale.

programme.desc: In Somerset, Mickey from the busy seaside town of Hove is ready to fulfil a long-held dream of escaping to the countryside. She wants a property with lots of land for her big, beloved rescue dog, and Jules Hudson is there to answer her prayers with a line-up of standout properties that fit the bill. Jules also tries his hand at traditional screen printing when he visits a small studio specialising in designs inspired by nature.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian rail journey continues in the Dutch-speaking region of Flanders. He discovers Antwerp's historic diamond industry and visits a Van Eyck masterpiece in Ghent.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael takes a luxury, panoramic Shimakaze train to the beautiful Shima peninsula. He visits Nara, the ancient capital of Japan, and encounters one of the largest bronze Buddha statues in the world.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Leaving Seoul aboard South Korea's high-speed KTX train, Michael travels south through the heart of the country to explore how rail has shaped both its economy and identity.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: The hunt for the nation's Mastermind continues as Clive Myrie tests four more contenders on their specialist subjects and general knowledge. The subjects are the jazz pianist Bill Evans, Georges Remi and Tintin, William Morris, and Torchwood.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Both Gus and Tippy struggle with dilemmas, while Daisy's plan to wow the showcase attendees falls apart at every step; Louis receives a distressing phone call.

programme.title: The Secret Life of Elephants
programme.desc: The emotional and dramatic lives of elephants in Kenya's Samburu reserve. As the day begins, there is great excitement in one elephant family when a new baby named Breeze is born, but her first few weeks look set to be the most dangerous of her life. Meanwhile, experts Iain and Saba Douglas-Hamilton have a big challenge: fitting a radio collar to a three-tonne female elephant. With Sarah Parish.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: At Warwick Castle, fascinating finds include a bizarre cigar lighter, a model guillotine made by French POWs, and a life drawing of spectacular quality.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Claire Rawle and Jonathan Pratt find a selection of antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Claire discovers an arts and crafts bowl which may have belonged to George Bernard Shaw's wife, and Jonathan gets excited when he finds a drawing by the artist Dame Laura Knight. Paul Martin takes a trip underground as he explores the intriguing Royston cave which boasts medieval carvings.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Cheryl Baker, Chris Bavin, Axel Blake and Sophie Willan take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Continuing his Belgian tour, Michael Portillo heads to the beach resort of Knokke-Heist and the world's longest coastal tram, which enjoys splendid views across the North Sea.

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo explores the stunning Dee valley, home to North Wales' longest river, from wintering birds along the estuary, to displaying black grouse on moorland, rare freshwater-pearl mussels, and spawning sea trout.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian rail journey continues in the Dutch-speaking region of Flanders. He discovers Antwerp's historic diamond industry and visits a Van Eyck masterpiece in Ghent.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael takes a luxury, panoramic Shimakaze train to the beautiful Shima peninsula. He visits Nara, the ancient capital of Japan, and encounters one of the largest bronze Buddha statues in the world.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Leaving Seoul aboard South Korea's high-speed KTX train, Michael travels south through the heart of the country to explore how rail has shaped both its economy and identity.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind

programme.title: Lightning

programme.title: The Secret Life of Elephants
programme.desc: The emotional and dramatic lives of elephants in Kenya's Samburu reserve. As the day begins, there is great excitement in one elephant family when a new baby named Breeze is born, but her first few weeks look set to be the most dangerous of her life. Meanwhile, experts Iain and Saba Douglas-Hamilton have a big challenge: fitting a radio collar to a three-tonne female elephant. With Sarah Parish.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Flog It!

Paul Martin takes a trip underground as he explores the intriguing Royston cave which boasts medieval carvings.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug and Cat Noir
programme.desc: Monarch is faced with a unique opportunity that he refuses to miss. The world is about to be reconfigured, and no one seems able to prevent it, for the Kwamis have made a choice that completely freed Marinette and Adrien from their responsibilities.

programme.title: Jamie Johnson FC
programme.desc: Jordan feels the pressure while Chelsea and Charlie get away from the Academy.

programme.title: The Next Step: Cheer
programme.desc: Kenzie finalizes the team, and Layla worries she won't make mat.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Join the CBBC crew for all your favourite programmes. Tune into CBBC every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel. Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Chicory running the show backstage and singing about how she, the Belgian Endive, was discovered in the song `Chicory, Dickory, Dock'.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Children's animation series. Bing makes Sula a picture with all her favourite things on it. It just needs one last jewel to be stuck on but the glue spills all over the picture. Sparkles save the day.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies have fun bumping into one another and watch the children in Tummy Tales play in bumper cars.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: A trip to the park is the perfect opportunity for Tee to fly his kite and for Mo to try out her new pop-up tent. However, kite flying and tent popping can be trickier than they seem.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato and Carrot use chopsticks to stop Evil Pea in her evil balloon.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are performing a puppet show for Flop, Amma and Pando when Coco and Charlie arrive. Coco wants to join in with her Rainbow Fairy Mice. Sula tells a frustrated Bing that you have to be loud when you do a show. But Bing is a little too loud, and when Coco joins the show, Bing's loud Biteysaurus isn't welcome, as he is hurting Sula's ears!

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo's got the perfect top for digging up the bones of a Swoppitus Rex. But could an actual dinosaur still be alive under Jackson's field, or is it a case of mistaken identity?

programme.title: Hey Duggee's Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Betty recalls the first time she pushed the red button, the Squirrels explore a range of emotions with Happy, Angry Bull tells a not-so-sleepy bedtime story, and the gerbils sing.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels become enchanted when they hear a piece of music from Duggee's choir. They quickly learn what makes a choir.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo finds a toy turtle and wants to take him home, but Dad says it's not the right thing to do.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Bingo drops Mum's breakfast-in-bed on the floor, she's convinced she's ruined her big day. Bluey finds a way to cheer her sister up and they put on a show about how Mum and Dad first met.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Ubercorn is excited to see the Northern Lights but Glitch's Grunge-Grimbler covers them with smoke.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Grumpy Owl
programme.desc: When Old Brown takes Peter's journal, Peter must choose between saving his most precious possession and saving a friend.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts help clean up a beach after a storm, but things get complicated when the resident fiddler crabs hijack the Gup-D.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: One and her chums carefully cross the pattern puzzle paths over the magic moats to get to the Pattern Palace. Learn about patterns with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: A, N and T sit down for a nice picnic, but soon everyone is getting tickled, the food starts disappearing and N gets the blame.

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: Nick is performing at a special birthday party and needs a poptastic list of songs to sing. He can't believe his eyes when the birthday girl calls up to check on the list, and together they write today's Popcast song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Hey! Diddle, Diddle
programme.desc: Billy and Sillies perform a little hoedown to the tune of Hey Diddle Diddle.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella plays funny games with Lift using her colourful towel. Together, they visit Hettie in the Ice Disco, who uses a big towel to make up a new dance - the Butterfly Boogie!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop teaches Bing and Pando how to blow bubbles. Pando soon gets the hang of it, but Bing struggles and gets frustrated. Finally, Bing blows a big bubble and is outraged when Pando pops it.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `rocket' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We explore all sorts of rockets, a big rocket, a tiny rocket and a shiny rocket. We see a blast off rocket, a flying rocket and a rocket zooming to the moon. There's even a banana rocket.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Barnaby gets upset when he can't contribute to Lu's silly soup.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.sub-title: What's It Like to be a Tree?
programme.desc: Kiri and Lou try out being trees, which is mostly about keeping still, and make a very large new friend named Moa.

programme.title: Froglets
programme.desc: Ziff, Pipp and Button don their spacesuits to become the first frogs to walk on the moon.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos see a man putting something inside his tent, and they get carried away putting more and more things inside. Meanwhile, Peekaboo has managed to get stuck inside the teapot. Can Peekaboo get out without being spotted by the Twirlywoos?

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin's friend teaches him about celebrating Eid and together they enjoy a big feast. The Tumbles are also preparing a meal, but there's one guest who can't wait to start eating.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po are outside in the cold when they spot the Tiddlytubbies who are inside and warm. The Teletubbies put on hats, gloves and scarves to keep warm.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Elias, Eliza, Billie, Enya, Isobel, Teddy and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside everyone's bags are plastic tubs which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding baby appropriate plastic tub inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby!

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.desc: Lisa and the children tend to their sick dollies and sign and sing 'Miss Polly Had a Dolly'.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Aaron, Joda and Isla are on a new garden adventure, this time looking for plants called weeds, which grow in the most surprising of places.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca and Murphy are trying to come up with a new move to perfect their competition dance routine. They take a break for an ice-cold monster shake and find time to create a bottle penguin. The Mini Monsters demonstrate how to make everlasting sandcastles, and Rebecca's aunt calls from space - where she has made some alien friends. The Recycling Express arrives with empty tissue boxes, inspiring a mega monster make - a funny pineapple puppet.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey pretends to run a taxi service, where her first passenger is Businessman Dad, who urgently needs to get to the airport.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels see Duggee reading about wise animals and wish to learn more about what it means to be wise.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Come and join the number jam, it's counting with Duggee.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.sub-title: Woolly Bench and Giant Spiderweb Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the gardens in the city to the bus stop to catch a bus. On the way they have a concert under a giant spiderweb, sit on a woolly bench, spot a rollercoaster and even wash a giant greenhouse!

programme.title: My First Festivals
programme.desc: Following seven-year-old Iqra as she and her family prepare for the celebration Eid (Eid Al-Adha). With lots of things to do before the big day, Iqra starts her journey by visiting her local mosque to meet the Imam to find out more about Eid. Traditionally family members get each other a secret gift for Eid. After buying a secret present for her dad and a new dress, she joins her mum and grandma by helping them to cook up some delicious special food for her community.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Brunsli Biscuits are a traditional Swiss recipe that taste and smell very Christmassy. Tilly adds spices such as ground nutmeg to her chocolate biscuit dough, before cutting it into snowflake shapes. With the help of her sister, Tilly decorates her biscuits with a beautiful white icing. Tilly's table has lots of Christmas decorations on it as well as the traditional flower of Switzerland, edelweiss. Let's hope Tilly's friends enjoy the taste of the biscuits!

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is at a building site and finds out how tracks work to help a digger move up and down mounds of earth. Maddie wears some special shoe attachments to show us how the digger's tracks work to stop it sinking into the soil. Then she attaches a special camera to the digger, to see it in action as it lifts the soil high into the dumper truck! Next, she visits a wellie factory to see how wellies are made.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Guide dog Kika and her owner Dr Amit are off to the Sticks 'n' Stuff shop with Amit's friend Alana, who is also blind, to help her buy a new white cane.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Owl Butterfly and Sunbittern
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie head into the heat of the butterfly house to meet the owl butterfly. They discover that this giant butterfly gets its name from the distinct patterns on its wings, which look like an owl's eye. Next, they visit the sunbittern.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Gram and Grand visit. Waffle loves Gram, he'll only leave his new doggy doormat on her command. Everyone sings Simon's new song and Waffle uses his doormat to stop Gram leaving.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona is in danger. She's tangled up in fishing twine, and the seagulls will be back soon. Mossy and Bernie try to free her, but can one little shrew save the day?

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: It's been raining, and Toad is resisting the urge to join in with Yukee and her friends' game of musical mud-squelching. However, he can't resist the muddy beat and is soon squelching with his friends.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Due to a typical McGinty mix-up, Triggs ends up competing in an obstacle race. He soon discovers that playing to win and playing for fun are two very different things.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are out doing their exercises when, suddenly, a cheetah called Chipo comes along with his dance crew. He criticises them for not being able to dance in time, and it turns out Duggee used to be part of the dance crew. Duggee was a great dancer, but he never stuck to the choreography and was always making up his own moves. But the Squirrels don't know what choreography is. The Rock Slippy Crew show off some impressive moves but keep criticising Duggee's moves.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Five is so good at hide and seek, she can find the others without looking up from her book. Learn how to add to five with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: P, A, R, T and Y need some help getting the party started. S tries her best but they really need someone extraordinary to liven things up and Exciting X soon gets the party popping with his superhero powers.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Maddie and her friends explore nature at the park, and Mr Tumble is an artist.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po run around in circles in the Home Dome. The Noo-noo joins in and the Teletubbies have fun chasing.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how wild flower meadows play an important role in the environment. They find out that both big and small patches of wild flowers can be great wildlife corridors for creatures to move around safely.

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Elvis must learn to tie his own laces quickly to achieve his PB in goalkeeping.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that all belong on the beach. Grace uses a very special machine to launch a lifeboat, shows off a machine that helps scientists and drives a sea tractor.

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: Dodge is determined to win the Class Star award, but with Cherry winning every week for being a superstar at everything, it's going to be tough! Dodge, Woofer and Jazz try to hatch a plan, but when Jazz and Dodge get swept up in their ideas, like getting Miss Collie a gift, they ignore Woofer's advice that there's a better way. Dodge brings Miss Collie a gift into the classroom from the playground and chaos ensues, leaving Dodge with no hope of winning the class star.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the baby Pea-Rexes must teach a lost baby BokChoydactyl how to fly so it can return to its parents. Unfortunately, baby Bokchoydactyls are terrible flyers.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Tracker finds trapped creatures in an underground tunnel after falling into a sinkhole.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Fish Out Of Water
programme.desc: Peter Rabbit wants to grow up to be just like his late father - he has big paws to fill. But with his father's journal and the help of his best friends Benjamin and Lily, Peter is ready for some fun and delightful new discoveries.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels have just got some new walkie-talkies and are having so much fun with them. Duggee calls the Squirrels to come for some blueberry juice, but as Tag is running, he trips over a car and falls through a secret door in the wall. Communicating through his walkie-talkie, Tag doesn't know where he is. He can see lots and lots of things around him, but no-one has any idea where this room is in the house.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Sunny is nervous that Baby Mouse will get hurt when she starts to take her first steps.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea thinks the Veggies won't like it when she covers the supermarket in ice, but instead they have the best snow day ever, so she creates a giant snowball to ruin their fun.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Nori and Dot are stuck in a tree house without a door. How can Mojo and Bo get them out? By sending the treehouse flying across town, that's how!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo finds a toy turtle and wants to take him home, but Dad says it's not the right thing to do.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Bingo drops Mum's breakfast-in-bed on the floor, she's convinced she's ruined her big day. Bluey finds a way to cheer her sister up and they put on a show about how Mum and Dad first met.

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Gadabout and Pearl need Zog's help to catch a stink beetle for an urgent cure. Little do they know, Zog has become a taxi for the gnomes.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum uses Ava's doubling machine by mistake, doubling all his recycling. But with Dr Ollie's help, it becomes Cyclo the Recycling Robot, giving the children lots of fun.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is at Mrs Hobbs' house until the Brooklyn-Bells return from holiday. He acts like a cat to please her and helpfully washes up his bowl, but accidentally leaves the taps on.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi wishes she could see a real dinosaur. Slurpy appears, causing chaos in the museum.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: - Spring:It's Time to Visit the Market
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran visit the market early in the morning to buy some fish. But before they can collect their fish from Navid the fishmonger, they get distracted helping their friends. They choose a new ball for Monty and help Charlene choose the perfect headwraps. Navid saves their fish for them, and JoJo learns that the market is just like a family - the people are kind and look out for each other.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah needs some new shoes, but picks out a very unusual pair.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major invents a Clanger finder, but it is not very useful - it always finds the Clanger who is holding it. Major is disappointed - he hadn't thought of that. But Granny comes up with a better suggestion to keep him busy: he could mend their clock, which hasn't worked for some time. Major Clanger has a think, then brightens up immediately. He can do better than that - he will build a new clock, and it will be his best invention ever. But it turns out the others don't agree.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: BaBa becomes friends with a Tweetie - a BaBa Tweetie. MeMo would like a Tweetie friend too, so at Play Time, MeMo befriends a new toy, MeMo Dog. MeMo loves playing with MeMo Dog, while BaBa enjoys playing with BaBa Tweetie. MeMo takes MeMo Dog to Yummy Time to show the Yummies. Yummy Dog loves MeMo Dog too and takes care of MeMo Dog for MeMo. MeMo lets MeMo Dog stay with Yummy Dog.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The ball is bouncing through the garden and lands straight on Upsy Daisy's bed. The bed carries the ball all the way to Upsy Daisy. Upsy Daisy tucks the ball in so it can have a rest.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: It's time to say goodnight with Kylie Minogue as she reads a tale especially for you.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving, the friends form teams and play American football. Ross and Monica relive their sibling rivalry by going head-to-head as opposing team captains. Rachel is upset that Ross didn't choose her for his team, and Joey tries to outwit his foe Chandler. Meanwhile, the turkey is left stewing while the losing team calls for a rematch.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel wonders if she'll ever get another job after she quits Central Perk, Joey's new job selling Christmas trees upsets Phoebe, and Ross accidentally breaks a little girl's leg.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Can't Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Ross is suspicious about Rachel's possible job offer from a good-looking stranger, while Chandler can't remember which of Joey's many attractive sisters he kissed at a party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Jealousy
programme.desc: Ross becomes increasingly jealous of Rachel's sexy new colleague, Mark, and gets the wrong end of the stick when he spies on Rachel at her office. Meanwhile, Monica is enamoured with a sexy busboy who writes love poems for her, and Joey gets himself into trouble by faking his CV in order to get a part in a Broadway musical.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Monica and Richard Are Just Friends
programme.desc: Monica bumps into Richard in a video store and they decide to work on their friendship, but the more time they spend together, the more sexual their relationship becomes. Meanwhile, Phoebe dates an athlete who unintentionally keeps exposing himself by wearing loose shorts, and Rachel agrees to read `The Shining' if Joey will read `Little Women'.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe is reunited with her former singing partner, Leslie, a failed television commercial jingle writer who wants to use Phoebe's signature song `Smelly Cat' in an advertisement for cat litter. Meanwhile, Ross becomes jealous when Rachel agrees to go to a fashion lecture with Mark, a former colleague who has just left for a new job, and Chandler meets a beautiful woman, Ginger, who has a prosthetic leg.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross and Rachel Take a Break
programme.desc: Rachel's job prevents her from celebrating her anniversary with Ross. Wanting more time to focus on her new job, she suggests the two take a break from their relationship. Convinced that Rachel has dumped him for one of her workmates, Ross hooks up with a woman from the copy shop that Joey and Chandler have been after.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries to prevent Rachel from learning his disturbing secret. Meanwhile, Monica, Phoebe, Chandler and Joey spend a night eavesdropping.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Without the Ski Trip
programme.desc: Ross and Rachel have been fighting for the group's attention since the break up. Rachel invites everyone except Ross to her family cabin in the mountains, but on the way to the cabin, they run out of petrol. Everyone wants to call Ross for help, but Rachel won't let them.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Hypnosis Tape
programme.desc: Phoebe is upset when her 18-year-old half-brother, Frank Jr, wants to marry his attractive home economics teacher, who is more than 25 years older than him. Elsewhere, Monica receives a tip from an admirer worth a small fortune, and a hypnosis tape intended to assist Chandler give up smoking has a rather strange effect on him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Tiny T-Shirt
programme.desc: Rachel's former colleague Mark asks her out on a date. When Rachel invites Ross to her apartment, he thinks she wants to get back together, but she just wants to return his things. Later, as Rachel and Mark return from their date, Ross spies on them through the peephole of Joey and Chandler's door. Meanwhile, Monica dates a millionaire, and Joey is cast in a play and clashes with the female lead.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica inherits a beautiful dollhouse that is too elaborate to play with, so Phoebe makes her own, but it meets a sad end. Meanwhile, Joey thinks he's found his true love, and Rachel's boss dates Chandler, placing her in a dangerous situation.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With a Chick and a Duck
programme.desc: When Monica's millionaire friend Pete buys a restaurant and asks Monica to be the head chef, she is faced with a difficult decision. Meanwhile, Ross makes a sacrifice for Rachel, and Joey buys Chandler an unusual pet.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The friends go to the opening night of one of Joey's plays. When Rachel finds out that Ross is bringing a date, she finds one of her own. Ross tries to convince Rachel that her date is mentally unbalanced. Meanwhile, Joey's play gets terrible reviews, but his co-star provides some comfort.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross develops a skin growth that doctors can't identify.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ultimate Fighting Champion
programme.desc: Monica finds herself dating a guy who wants to be the Ultimate Fighting Champion. Meanwhile, Chandler finds his male boss's attentions just a little too friendly, and the group overhear a scandalous conversation at Central Perk.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is devastated after discovering his family lied to him about his guest appearance on a TV sports show.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray uses an active listening technique learned in parenting class on his stubborn parents.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray reluctantly agrees to take Robert out on the anniversary of his divorce, but is disturbed by the `brotherly bonding thing' that develops.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray wants to teach Ally not to be a quitter, so he resumes the piano lessons he dropped 25-years earlier. Then, he wonders why he ever gave up in the first place.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray's guilt catches up with him when he tricks Debra into letting him play a round of golf.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: At Frank and Marie's anniversary party, Ray discovers that his parents split up for a year and only got back together after Ray fell off his bike and broke his arm. He can't help feeling guilty.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving, the friends form teams and play American football. Ross and Monica relive their sibling rivalry by going head-to-head as opposing team captains. Rachel is upset that Ross didn't choose her for his team, and Joey tries to outwit his foe Chandler. Meanwhile, the turkey is left stewing while the losing team calls for a rematch.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel wonders if she'll ever get another job after she quits Central Perk, Joey's new job selling Christmas trees upsets Phoebe, and Ross accidentally breaks a little girl's leg.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Can't Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Ross is suspicious about Rachel's possible job offer from a good-looking stranger, while Chandler can't remember which of Joey's many attractive sisters he kissed at a party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Jealousy
programme.desc: Ross becomes increasingly jealous of Rachel's sexy new colleague, Mark, and gets the wrong end of the stick when he spies on Rachel at her office. Meanwhile, Monica is enamoured with a sexy busboy who writes love poems for her, and Joey gets himself into trouble by faking his CV in order to get a part in a Broadway musical.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Monica and Richard Are Just Friends
programme.desc: Monica bumps into Richard in a video store and they decide to work on their friendship, but the more time they spend together, the more sexual their relationship becomes. Meanwhile, Phoebe dates an athlete who unintentionally keeps exposing himself by wearing loose shorts, and Rachel agrees to read `The Shining' if Joey will read `Little Women'.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe is reunited with her former singing partner, Leslie, a failed television commercial jingle writer who wants to use Phoebe's signature song `Smelly Cat' in an advertisement for cat litter. Meanwhile, Ross becomes jealous when Rachel agrees to go to a fashion lecture with Mark, a former colleague who has just left for a new job, and Chandler meets a beautiful woman, Ginger, who has a prosthetic leg.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross and Rachel Take a Break
programme.desc: Rachel's job prevents her from celebrating her anniversary with Ross. Wanting more time to focus on her new job, she suggests the two take a break from their relationship. Convinced that Rachel has dumped him for one of her workmates, Ross hooks up with a woman from the copy shop that Joey and Chandler have been after.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries to prevent Rachel from learning his disturbing secret. Meanwhile, Monica, Phoebe, Chandler and Joey spend a night eavesdropping.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Without the Ski Trip
programme.desc: Ross and Rachel have been fighting for the group's attention since the break up. Rachel invites everyone except Ross to her family cabin in the mountains, but on the way to the cabin, they run out of petrol. Everyone wants to call Ross for help, but Rachel won't let them.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Hypnosis Tape
programme.desc: Phoebe is upset when her 18-year-old half-brother, Frank Jr, wants to marry his attractive home economics teacher, who is more than 25 years older than him. Elsewhere, Monica receives a tip from an admirer worth a small fortune, and a hypnosis tape intended to assist Chandler give up smoking has a rather strange effect on him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Tiny T-Shirt
programme.desc: Rachel's former colleague Mark asks her out on a date. When Rachel invites Ross to her apartment, he thinks she wants to get back together, but she just wants to return his things. Later, as Rachel and Mark return from their date, Ross spies on them through the peephole of Joey and Chandler's door. Meanwhile, Monica dates a millionaire, and Joey is cast in a play and clashes with the female lead.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica inherits a beautiful dollhouse that is too elaborate to play with, so Phoebe makes her own, but it meets a sad end. Meanwhile, Joey thinks he's found his true love, and Rachel's boss dates Chandler, placing her in a dangerous situation.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With a Chick and a Duck
programme.desc: When Monica's millionaire friend Pete buys a restaurant and asks Monica to be the head chef, she is faced with a difficult decision. Meanwhile, Ross makes a sacrifice for Rachel, and Joey buys Chandler an unusual pet.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The friends go to the opening night of one of Joey's plays. When Rachel finds out that Ross is bringing a date, she finds one of her own. Ross tries to convince Rachel that her date is mentally unbalanced. Meanwhile, Joey's play gets terrible reviews, but his co-star provides some comfort.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross develops a skin growth that doctors can't identify.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ultimate Fighting Champion
programme.desc: Monica finds herself dating a guy who wants to be the Ultimate Fighting Champion. Meanwhile, Chandler finds his male boss's attentions just a little too friendly, and the group overhear a scandalous conversation at Central Perk.

programme.desc: Dark matter is one of the greatest mysteries of the universe facing scientists today. Could it be the cause of supermassive black holes and mass extinctions?

programme.title: America's Most Secret: Structures
programme.desc: Investigate America's most classified sites and their covert activities. Venture deep inside the government's most sensitive, top secret locations and analyse their purpose.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored
programme.desc: Justin and Emiliano investigate a legendary lost mine in the mountains of Arizona. It is said to contain over 200 million dollars' worth of gold.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they build Formula 1 racing cars, how do they make the Leatherman, America's best-selling multi-tool, and how do they create classic Finnish Aalto glassware?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.sub-title: Hats; Longest Road Tunnel; Snake Catcher
programme.desc: How do the descendants of the Mayans make the finest Panama hats; how do they keep drivers safe in the world's longest road tunnel; and how do they catch Florida's escaped pythons?

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers
programme.desc: Upholsterer Craig is apprehensive about repairing an extremely rare leather sofa, not wanting to make a mistake. Pinball expert Mark gets a surprise whilst fixing a classic machine.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.sub-title: Birth of the Monster Black Holes
programme.desc: New discoveries reveal an astonishing supermassive black hole that was born during the earliest days of the cosmos, and finding out how this giant grew so large so quickly may help to explain the very formation of the universe itself.

programme.title: Mysteries at the Museum
programme.sub-title: Mystery of the Spinning Statue; the Unwanted Elephant; Pirates of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Don Wildman investigates a statue which seemed to have taken on a life of its own, a strange skull from a beast that once was the star of the Big Top, and a small carving of an infamous dictator.

programme.title: Mysteries at the Museum

programme.title: Mysteries of the Missing
programme.desc: In 2009, an Air France flight disappears, but could this tragedy happen again? After a huge area of Siberian forest is destroyed, it's a race to find the culprit.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Josh investigates the theft of one of the most iconic pieces of Hollywood memorabilia of all time, the ruby slippers worn by Judy Garland in the classic 1939 film `The Wizard of Oz'.

programme.sub-title: Alfa Romeo Spider & Renault Alpine GTA

programme.title: Combat Dealers

programme.title: Blowing Up History
programme.sub-title: Sunken City of Helike: The Hunt for the Real Atlantis
programme.desc: In 373 B.C., the Greek city of Helike vanished; archaeologists believe they've found the ruins of the great lost metropolis; using CGI, experts re-create the city's final moments and reveal its links to the greatest lost land of all -- Atlantis.

programme.title: Blowing Up History
programme.sub-title: Olous: Lost City of the Ancients

programme.title: How the Universe Works

programme.title: Motor Pickers

programme.title: Motor Pickers

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: Classic Cars
programme.desc: Paul has paid just over £15,000 for an Alfa Romeo 2000 GTV that appears to be in great shape. However, Drew is horrified when he learns it's an automatic.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around people's sheds and garages in search of automotive bargains. Today, on the Cornish coast, Henry and Sam spend the day surrounded by vintage vehicles with genteel pedigrees including a the first ever Lotus Elan, and a pre-war Sunbeam. In Oxfordshire, Sam falls for a a Midget racer - but the restoration is a daunting task.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: The boys are in Oxfordshire, and Mark's shed is more of a boneyard for giants. Everything Henry and Sam look at here is oversized and likely over-priced, but Henry likes a tractor in blue and Sam falls for a Midget racer in a hedge.

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Big!
programme.desc: Richard ventures two kilometres beneath the Alps into the world's longest underground railway connection, the Brenner Base Tunnel, riding some giant machines.

programme.title: Roadworthy Rescues
programme.desc: Derek and Sean Bieri head back to Zweifel Motors in the Black Hills of South Dakota to revive an Oldsmobile station wagon Derek has been eyeing for 16 years.

programme.title: Bad Chad Customs
programme.desc: Chad builds a custom bare metal truck to give away at his Rockabilly car show. With the tightest of budgets, the team builds the truck from scratch, and Chad's wild ideas keep them in overdrive.

programme.title: Ant Anstead: Born Mechanic
programme.desc: Master mechanic Ant Anstead restores and sells classic cars with a hope to turn some profit to help fund the renovation of the 500-year-old farmhouse he bought for his mum and dad.

programme.title: Massive Engineering Mistakes
programme.desc: Tragedy strikes a bustling Canadian shopping mall when a parking garage suddenly collapses resulting in a fatal disaster; expert engineers use the latest technology to investigate and uncover a deadly design flaw that could have been prevented.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: Classic Cars
programme.desc: Paul and Drew check out a BMW 323i that's been sitting in a barn for 12 years. Drew wants to give the car a tasteful revamp, but Paul has a flashier plan.

programme.desc: The Raneys help the Johnson family who have no drinking water or food sources. Their dogs have been killed by a mystery predator and worry they are next.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys assist two Montana homesteaders who face being trapped on their property. When a medical emergency strikes, the Raneys face their first ever failed rescue.

programme.title: Outback Opal Hunters
programme.desc: Six teams travel across Australia's unique and vast locations to find the country's most exclusive and iconic gemstones, especially the opal.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Josh Gates and his team investigate the realms beyond the unknown.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown

programme.title: Naked and Afraid XL
programme.desc: After a shock exit, the remaining survivalists face a brutal two-day water extraction journey. Electric eels, anaconda and caiman all pose threats in this finale.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid XL
programme.desc: Heading deep into Africa's scorching Selati Basin, thirteen all-stars attempt to roam this dangerous terrain for 40 days. With scarce resources, the competition for food is fierce especially with hungry predators on their trail.

programme.desc: Bart feels sorry for Mrs Krabapple after Skinner stands her up again to attend to his mother's whims, so Lisa suggests he should nominate her for Teacher of the Year. However, Skinner worries that he could lose her if she wins.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Greek restaurant Yanni's in Seattle, where he clashes with defiant owner Peter, a chef who is so set in his ways that he hasn't allowed any changes to his menu since the business opened in 1984. His own family are concerned that the restaurant is becoming trapped in the past, but Peter is determined to stick to his guns.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Joe DiDomizio is the president and CEO of Hudson Group, a travel retailer that operates newsstands, bookshops, cafés and speciality stores in 70 airports and other transportation terminals throughout North America.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Utah Jazz CEO Greg Miller's team spirit is tested when he is asked to be a human basketball hoop while he is undercover.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: A woman who has beaten cancer wants to be able to live the rest of her life to the fullest, and the boys must help her find the dream space to do just that. Jonathan turns her home into a showstopper and Drew's house hunt ends up close to square one.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Laura and James, Sarah and Dan, and Edward and Carol compete to throw the best dinner party in Surrey, in an edition featuring rapping, monobrows and surprising revelations.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bromley, Ryan and Julie's food fails to impress, with one guest comparing the main to vomit! Can Jo and Alex or Michelle and Craig do better with their fine dining and Caribbean menus?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Ex-footballer Steven, 26, tries so hard to pull off the wedding of his dreams, but when he's confronted with his furious bride-to-be Kaleigh on their wedding day, does he finally realise he's made a huge mistake? Steven decides to have their wedding at Thorpe Park, but his idea of putting Kaleigh on the Stealth ride before they've even tied the knot comes crashing down when she refuses to do it.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One at a Naval College
programme.desc: Essex boy Andrew, 27, is a groom torn between two worlds. On one side is his former boss in the Royal Navy and bride-to-be Amanda, who wants Andrew to man up and take charge of the wedding. Although Amanda left the navy three years ago, she still gives the orders in their relationship, but in his desperation to please her, will he make the wrong decision?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Rick Forman, founder and CEO of New Jersey-based Forman Mills, a discount clothing superstore, goes incognito among his own staff posing as a contestant on a television reality show.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Stella & Dot CEO Jessica Herrin goes under cover to find the real gems working in her company, and learns one employee's true feelings about corporate management.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Cambridge, New York, where ex-military man John Imhof and his wife, Tina, own and operate the allegedly haunted Cambridge Hotel.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: The first part of a two-part visit to the Juniper Hill Inn in Windsor, Vermont, a place that looks like a million dollars but smells of raw sewage. Gordon meets the inn's wealthy owner, Robert Dean II, and uncovers some shocking treatment towards the staff. Gordon quickly realises that it's going to need something approaching a miracle if he's going to have a chance of saving the place.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: Recently widowed, Rosanne is selling the suburban home she bought with her late partner Joe and is looking for a cosy downtown property. In order to get the most from the sale, Jonathan steps in to transform the property.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Essex, Karen and DJ Paul, outdoorsy couple Lucy and her partner Paul, and Pat and her husband Tino compete in a week featuring a guest who is left hungry and an unconventional dessert.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Little Alex Horne risks raising Greg Davies' hackles with a task in celebration of himself. Steve Pemberton has an expletive-laden row with a bin, and a novel use for a cow's ear.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, as Sean Lock and Jason Manford go up against Jon Richardson and Roisin Conaty. Sam Simmons joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: It's Adam's birthday and as he arrives home, a silver Mercedes almost runs him over. Inside, is Sheila Bloom. She is famously obsessed with her Mercedes, and after some catty remarks about Adam's `Yugo', she speeds off. Dad reveals that he used to have a thing with Sheila years ago, the boys can't believe it and Mum's not happy. Later, the family abandon dinner and go for a Chinese, but Sheila is there.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Adam comes home to find the house looking immaculate, with classical music playing in the living room, Mum's posh bowls on the dinner table, Dad in a smart suit, and most mysteriously - the table is set for five. The doorbell rings and in comes Tanya Green, a girl Mum has been trying to fix up with Adam for months. What follows is possibly the most excruciating family dinner scene of all time.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Jeremy tries to wow Toni with his new job in a music studio while Mark gets passed over for promotion in favour of Sophie. They then attempt to solve each other's problems with the help of phone stalking and pepper spray.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Death and terminal illness prove to be the keys to romantic success for Mark and Jeremy. Jeremy gets in with the Christians, while Mark finds a funeral to be the perfect venue for a first date with Sophie.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers on Holiday
programme.desc: Ian needs help from Sketch to cover his Magaluf monstrosity, Alice helps Aston with his ugly Ibiza sunset, and Jay gives Callum a portrait to remember on his final holiday before he joins the army.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Little Alex Horne risks raising Greg Davies' hackles with a task in celebration of himself. Steve Pemberton has an expletive-laden row with a bin, and a novel use for a cow's ear.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Three previously featured bosses ask employees to go under cover for a second look inside their organisations.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Jeff Platt, president and CEO of entertainment and fitness business Sky Zone, a chain of indoor trampoline parks, goes undercover in his own company posing as a contestant on a reality TV competition who is trying to win an action-adventure holiday.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.sub-title: A Home for All in the Family
programme.desc: James and Ada are on the hunt for a spacious family home with a flat for Grandma and Grandpa, but they need to spruce up their current house of oddities before they can make the move.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Lee and Tash, Judy and Will, and Jess and Dale compete to host the best dinner party in Stockport, in an edition featuring vanilla cured salmon, some unwanted guests, and a shocking tattoo.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Air stewardess Penny and her partner Chris, bling-tastic Tatia and her husband Mick, and youngsters Lewis and Krystal compete to host the best dinner party in Essex.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: The pressure is on for 30-year-old sci-fi fanatic Charles as he tries to impress his more classy bride-to-be, 26-year-old Lauren. Originally both from Yorkshire, they met five years ago when they worked behind a bar in Newcastle and have been inseparable ever since. Lauren is an art teacher with very sophisticated tastes and prefers the finer things in life, whereas Charles is an only child who has never grown up.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Worst Hen Do Ever
programme.desc: Tomboy Anna, 31, tries to plan the perfect day for fiancee Sian, 24. The couple have been inseparable since they met in a bar, and four months later Anna knew Sian was `the one' and promptly asked the question.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Armando Montelongo, the chief executive officer of Armando Montelongo Companies, hires a body double to help conceal his identity while under cover.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Gigi Butler, the chief executive officer of Gigi's Cupcakes, struggles with the pressures of cake decorating while under cover.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: This programme sees Mr Ramsay's greatest challenge to date, as he continues to uncover problem after problem at the Juniper Hill Inn in Windsor, Vermont. He tries to help the staff communicate with owners Robert and Ari, but after catching one of them in a shocking lie, Gordon feels he has no choice but to abandon ship.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay checks in to the River Rock Inn in Milford, Pennsylvania. Owner Ken Pisciotta is in over his head and drowning in debt and unable to get any guests to stay in his neglected establishment.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: A suburban couple is ready to move back downtown with their two teenage daughters. Jonathan takes charge of transforming the family's lacklustre home into a showpiece, while Drew leads the hunt for a dreamy downsize.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sports car manufacturers Charlotte and Neil kick off with some tip-top cuisine. Newly-weds Luke and Alice plan a panto-themed menu, and Hardeep and Sareena host a `bhangra bonanza'.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: It's the grand final, but before a new champion gets the honour of clutching Greg Davies' gilded bonce to their proud chest, Nick Mohammed makes a magical prediction.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: When Jonny discovers Adam's old diary, he learns a terrible secret. When Adam was 11, he secretly dumped Jonny's beloved `Pandy' panda bear toy in the dustbin, never to be seen again.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: The boys are excited: grandma is coming for dinner, and tonight she's bringing her new boyfriend. Lou Morris is a horrible, vain man, who sports a trilby, a Hitler-ish moustache, and is obsessed with cleanliness. programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Mark's lack of success with Sophie is compensated when he makes a new friend, Daryl, at the office. Meanwhile, Jeremy meets Gog, an old schoolfriend who he used to bully, but is now successful, and may even have a job for him.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Jeff is going on a work trip with Sophie, and Mark - fearful that they will sleep together - becomes desperate to stop them. Meanwhile, Jeremy has become uncontrollably jealous of any man that Nancy, his new girlfriend, talks to, looks at, walks past, or breathes towards.
